title: fixing-accessibility
purpose: Review and fix UI accessibility violations with targeted, minimal patches covering ARIA attributes, semantic HTML, keyboard access, focus management, and form error linking.
when: When adding or changing buttons, links, inputs, menus, dialogs, tabs, dropdowns, forms, validation, error states, focus states, focus trapping, modal behavior, icon-only controls, or hover-only interactions.

title: fixing-metadata
purpose: Audit and fix page metadata including titles, descriptions, canonical URLs, Open Graph tags, Twitter cards, favicons, structured data, and locale settings.
when: When adding or changing page titles, descriptions, canonical URLs, robots directives, Open Graph or Twitter card metadata, favicons, app icons, manifest, theme-color, structured data (JSON-LD), locale, alternate languages, or canonical routing.

title: fixing-motion-performance
purpose: Diagnose and fix animation performance issues including layout thrashing, composite layer promotion, FLIP patterns, scroll-linked motion, and reduced-motion preferences.
when: When adding or changing UI animations (CSS, WAAPI, Motion, rAF, GSAP), refactoring janky interactions, implementing scroll-linked motion, animating layout properties, or reviewing components that use will-change, transforms, or layout measurement.

title: screen-reader-testing
purpose: Test web applications with screen readers (VoiceOver, NVDA, JAWS, TalkBack) covering navigation, forms, dynamic content, modals, live regions, and tab interfaces.
when: When validating screen reader compatibility, testing ARIA implementations, debugging assistive technology issues, verifying form accessibility, testing dynamic content announcements, or ensuring navigation accessibility.

title: ui-visual-validator
purpose: Verify UI modifications, design system compliance, and accessibility implementation through systematic visual analysis with evidence-based assessment methodology.
when: When verifying UI modifications visually, checking design system compliance, performing visual regression testing, or validating that accessibility changes render correctly.

title: wcag-audit-patterns
purpose: Audit web content against WCAG 2.2 guidelines across all four POUR principles with automated tooling, manual checks, and actionable remediation strategies.
when: When conducting accessibility audits, fixing WCAG violations, implementing accessible components, preparing for accessibility lawsuits, meeting ADA/Section 508 requirements, or achieving VPAT compliance.
